heading: State Digital Equity Capacity Grant Program
label: 1723 State Digital Equity Capacity Grant Program

children.label: A
children.text: the purpose of which is to promote the achievement of digital equity, support digital inclusion activities, and build capacity for efforts by States relating to the adoption of broadband by residents of those States;

children.label: B
children.text: through which the Assistant Secretary shall make grants to States in accordance with the requirements of this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: which shall ensure that States have the capacity to promote the achievement of digital equity and support digital inclusion activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The administering entity for a State shall make the State Digital Equity Plan of the State available for public comment for a period of not less than 30 days before the date on which the State submits an application to the Assistant Secretary under subsection (d)(2).

children.label: I
children.text: consider all comments received during the comment period described in subparagraph (A) with respect to the application (referred to in this subparagraph as the “comment period”); and

children.label: II
children.text: make any changes to the plan that the administering entity determines to be worthwhile; and

children.label: I
children.text: describe any changes pursued by the administering entity in response to comments received during the comment period; and

children.label: II
children.text: include a written response to each comment received during the comment period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning in the first fiscal year that begins after , the Assistant Secretary shall, in accordance with the requirements of this paragraph, award planning grants to States for the purpose of developing the State Digital Equity Plans of those States under this subsection.November 15, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: shall submit to the Assistant Secretary an application under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not have been awarded, at any time, a planning grant under this paragraph.

children.heading: Amount of grant
children.label: i Amount of grant
children.text: A planning grant awarded to an eligible State under this paragraph shall be determined according to the formula under subsection (d)(3)(A)(i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), with respect to a planning grant awarded to an eligible State under this paragraph, the State shall expend the grant funds during the 1-year period beginning on the date on which the State is awarded the grant funds.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: II Exception
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may grant an extension of not longer than 180 days with respect to the requirement under subclause (I).

children.heading: Challenge mechanism
children.label: iii Challenge mechanism
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall ensure that any eligible State to which a planning grant is awarded under this paragraph may appeal or otherwise challenge in a timely fashion the amount of the grant awarded to the State, as determined under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: To develop the State Digital Equity Plan of the State under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), to make subgrants to any of the entities described in paragraph (1)(D) to assist in the development of the State Digital Equity Plan of the State under this subsection.

children.label: II
children.text: If the administering entity for a State makes a subgrant described in subclause (I), the administering entity shall, with respect to the subgrant, provide to the State the assurances required under subsection (e).

children.label: I
children.text: 50 percent of the total grant amount shall be based on the population of the eligible State in proportion to the total population of all eligible States.

children.label: II
children.text: 25 percent of the total grant amount shall be based on the number of individuals in the eligible State who are members of covered populations in proportion to the total number of individuals in all eligible States who are members of covered populations.

children.label: aa
children.text: the annual inquiry of the Federal Communications Commission conducted under ;section 1302(b) of this title

children.text: the American Community Survey or, if necessary, other data collected by the Bureau of the Census;

children.label: cc
children.text: the NTIA Internet Use Survey, which is administered as the Computer and Internet Use Supplement to the Current Population Survey of the Bureau of the Census; and

children.label: dd
children.text: any other source that the Assistant Secretary, after appropriate notice and opportunity for public comment, determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Minimum award
children.label: ii Minimum award
children.text: The amount of a grant awarded to an eligible State under this subsection in a fiscal year shall be not less than 0.5 percent of the total amount made available to award grants to eligible States for that fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: to eligible States to which the Assistant Secretary has awarded grants under this subsection for that fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: in accordance with the formula described in clause (i).

children.heading: Data unavailable
children.label: iv Data unavailable
children.text: If, in a fiscal year, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico (referred to in this clause as “Puerto Rico”) is an eligible State and specific data for Puerto Rico is unavailable for a factor described in subclause (I), (II), or (II) of clause (i), the Assistant Secretary shall use the median data point with respect to that factor among all eligible States and assign it to Puerto Rico for the purposes of making any calculation under that clause for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: B Duration
children.text: With respect to a grant awarded to an eligible State under this subsection, the eligible State shall expend the grant funds during the 5-year period beginning on the date on which the eligible State is awarded the grant funds.

children.heading: Challenge mechanism
children.label: C Challenge mechanism
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall ensure that any eligible State to which a grant is awarded under this subsection may appeal or otherwise challenge in a timely fashion the amount of the grant awarded to the State, as determined under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), to update or maintain the State Digital Equity Plan of the State.

children.label: C

children.label: v

may establish additional reporting and information requirements for any recipient of a grant under this section.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant
children.label: h Supplement not supplant
children.text: A grant or subgrant awarded under this section shall supplement, not supplant, other Federal or State funds that have been made available to carry out activities described in this section.

not less than 5 percent to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, Indian Tribes, Alaska Native entities, and Native Hawaiian organizations to allow those tribes, entities, and organizations to carry out the activities described in this section; and

not less than 1 percent to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States that is not a State to enable those entities to carry out the activities described in this section.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: j Rules
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may prescribe such rules as may be necessary to carry out this section.
$60,000,000 for the award of grants under subsection (c)(3), which shall remain available until expended;
